topic: AI memory systems for LLMs and AI agents — industry frameworks, tools, and platforms

findings.claim: Letta (formerly MemGPT) is a full stateful agent platform with tiered memory blocks; latest v0.16.7 released March 2026 with 128k default context window
findings.evidence: Letta Server 0.16.7 Release Notes: 'Global context window default raised from 32k to 128k' and '173 commits since 0.16.6' (March 31, 2026). Letta repo has 23k GitHub stars, TypeScript migration (97.2% TS). Letta Code (memory-first coding agent) at v0.27.10 as of June 16, 2026 with 2.7k stars.
findings.kai_relevance: Letta is the most mature open-source agent platform with built-in memory — could serve as Kai-1's agent runtime if a full framework is preferred over a separate memory layer.

findings.claim: Letta supports model-agnostic deployment with PostgreSQL/SQLite backends, skills/subagents, MemFS git-tracked memory, and local CLI; no published LongMemEval score
findings.evidence: Letta README: 'Fully model-agnostic, though we recommend Opus 4.5 and GPT-5.2 for best performance'. Database: PostgreSQL/SQLite. OMEGA comparison table confirms Letta has no published LongMemEval score. Letta Cloud available at app.letta.com.
findings.kai_relevance: Letta's model-agnosticism and self-hosted option align with Kai-1's Qwen3.6-35B deployment, but lack of benchmark scores makes comparison with dedicated memory layers difficult.

findings.claim: Mem0 has 48k+ GitHub stars, raised $24M Series A (Oct 2025), YC-backed; self-reports 92.5% on LoCoMo and 94.4% on LongMemEval (April 2026)
findings.evidence: AgentMarketCap Apr 2026: 'Mem0 (the community favorite with 48,000+ GitHub stars and a $24M Series A)'. Mem0 State of AI Agent Memory 2026 (April 2026): 'LoCoMo: 92.5 ... LongMemEval: 94.4 ... BEAM (1M): 64.1'. Bench'd reports Mem0 managed platform self-reports 93.4% on LongMemEval but OSS edition scored 32.4% in independent test.
findings.kai_relevance: Critical discrepancy between managed and OSS Mem0 performance (94.4% vs 32.4% on LongMemEval) — Kai-1 self-hosted deployment would use OSS version, raising concerns about real-world performance.

findings.claim: Mem0's April 2026 token-efficient algorithm uses single-pass ADD-only extraction and multi-signal retrieval (semantic + BM25 + entity matching); biggest gains on temporal (+29.6 pts) and multi-hop (+23.1 pts)
findings.evidence: Mem0 State of AI Agent Memory 2026: 'Two architectural changes drove these results: Single-pass ADD-only extraction ... Multi-signal retrieval: three scoring passes in parallel — semantic similarity, keyword matching, and entity matching ... The two largest gains are on temporal queries (+29.6 points) and multi-hop reasoning (+23.1 points)'. Average tokens per query: ~6,956 (LoCoMo), ~6,787 (LongMemEval).
findings.kai_relevance: Mem0's multi-signal retrieval approach (fusing semantic + keyword + entity) is directly applicable to Kai-1's memory architecture design, and the 6.9k tokens/query efficiency is important for 32GB VPS deployment.

findings.claim: Zep/Graphiti uses temporal knowledge graphs; DMR 94.8% vs MemGPT 93.4%; LongMemEval 71.2%; LoCoMo 94.7%; requires Neo4j; YC W24 with $3.3M funding
findings.evidence: arXiv 2501.13956 (Jan 2025): 'Zep demonstrates superior performance (94.8% vs 93.4%)' on DMR. 'LongMemEval benchmark accuracy improvements of up to 18.5% while simultaneously reducing response latency by 90%'. Zep official site: LoCoMo 94.7% accuracy, 155ms latency, 5,760 tokens; LongMemEval 90.2% accuracy, 162ms latency, 4,408 tokens. OMEGA comparison: Zep LongMemEval 71.2% (different methodology). Generational.pub: 'Zep founded 2023, YC W24, $3.3M from Engineering Capital, Step Function, Vercel founders'.
findings.kai_relevance: Zep's temporal knowledge graph approach excels at tracking fact changes over time (e.g., user moved cities), but Neo4j dependency adds deployment complexity for Kai-1's 32GB VPS.

findings.claim: Graphiti (open source, Apache 2.0) runs on Neo4j/FalkorDB/Neptune; has built-in MCP server; Zep Cloud pricing: free 1K episodes then $25-475/mo
findings.evidence: Zep Graphiti page: 'Pluggable backends — Runs on Neo4j, FalkorDB, and Amazon Neptune'. 'MCP Server — Connect Graphiti directly to Claude, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible clients'. OMEGA comparison: 'Zep/Graphiti — Free (self-host). Cloud: Free 1K episodes, then $25-475/mo'.
findings.kai_relevance: Graphiti's MCP server makes it easy to integrate with Kai-1's agent stack, but Neo4j resource requirements may be prohibitive for a 32GB VPS running a ~22GB model.

findings.claim: LangMem SDK (released Feb 2025) provides semantic, episodic, and procedural memory for LangGraph agents; works with any storage backend; native LangGraph integration
findings.evidence: LangChain blog Feb 18, 2025: 'Today we're releasing the LangMem SDK, a library that helps your agents learn and improve through long-term memory ... provides tooling to extract information from conversations, optimize agent behavior through prompt updates'. PyPI: 'pip install -U langmem'. Works with InMemoryStore (dev) or AsyncPostgresStore (production).
findings.kai_relevance: LangMem is the natural choice if Kai-1 uses LangGraph for agent orchestration; native integration reduces architectural complexity.

findings.claim: OMEGA scores #1 on LongMemEval at 95.4% (GPT-4.1), fully local with zero cloud dependencies (SQLite + ONNX), Apache 2.0, free forever
findings.evidence: OMEGA official site: 'OMEGA leads with 95.4% on LongMemEval (vs Zep's 71.2%), runs fully local with zero cloud dependency, and requires no API keys'. 'Database: SQLite (built-in, zero config). Cloud Required: No. LongMemEval: 95.4%. License: Apache-2.0. Pricing: Free forever (open source)'. 'Task-averaged accuracy: 95.4% (raw: 466/500). Uses category-tuned answer prompts with GPT-4.1'. OMEGA blog (Feb 13, 2026): 'Single SQLite file running on a MacBook Pro. No cloud. No Neo4j. No external APIs for retrieval'.
findings.kai_relevance: OMEGA's local-first architecture and zero dependencies are ideal for Kai-1's 32GB VPS — no extra infrastructure, SQLite fits the resource budget alongside Qwen3.6-35B.

findings.claim: OMEGA provides 18 MCP tools, auto-capture hooks (session start/stop/prompt/tool use), graph relationships, temporal reasoning, intelligent forgetting, contradiction detection, and AES-256 encryption
findings.evidence: OMEGA comparison table: 'MCP Tools: 18. Semantic Search: Yes. Auto-Capture: Yes. Cross-Session Memory: Yes. Intelligent Forgetting: Yes. Graph Relationships: Yes. Temporal Reasoning: Yes. Checkpoint/Resume: Yes. Encryption at Rest: AES-256. Local-First/No Cloud: Yes. Zero External Dependencies: Yes. Contradiction Detection: Yes'. Token efficiency: ~1,500 tokens per query vs Zep ~5K-15K and Observational Memory ~70,000.
findings.kai_relevance: OMEGA's ~1,500 tokens/query efficiency and no external API dependencies make it the most resource-efficient option for Kai-1's constrained VPS, but it's a newer project with a smaller community than Mem0/Letta.

findings.claim: Mastra Observational Memory (OM) scores 94.87% on LongMemEval (gpt-5-mini) using in-context text compression with no vector database; TypeScript-first framework by Gatsby team
findings.evidence: Mastra OM research page: '94.87% on LongMemEval (gpt-5-mini) — highest recorded score on this benchmark by any system'. 'No vector database, no graph store — everything is text in context'. Mastra GitHub: 23k+ stars. $35M total funding. YC W25. Built by Gatsby.js team. MadAppGang guide: 'Four-tier memory architecture covering message history, working memory, semantic recall, and Observational Memory'.
findings.kai_relevance: Mastra OM's 'no infrastructure' approach is appealing for resource-constrained deployments, but ~70,000 tokens/query cost projection makes it expensive for Kai-1 scale (OMEGA-comparison figure).

findings.claim: Cognee provides GraphRAG-style AI memory via ECL pipeline (Extract-Cognify-Load); 17.8k GitHub stars; uses Neo4j/NetworkX + Qdrant/Weaviate
findings.evidence: AIBARS (June 2026): Cognee '17.8k Last Updated: June 15, 2026'. 'ECL (Extract, Cognify, Load) pipeline ... Graph and Vector Dual Storage'. OMEGA comparison: 'Database: Neo4j / NetworkX + Qdrant / Weaviate. Cloud Required: OpenAI key required for embeddings'. Cognee docs: 'Add memory in 5 lines of code using @cognee.agent_memory decorator'.
findings.kai_relevance: Cognee's dual graph+vector approach may be over-engineered for Kai-1's needs; Neo4j dependency adds deployment complexity on a 32GB VPS.

findings.claim: Weaviate Engram (launched GA April 2026) is a managed memory service with async extract-transform-commit pipeline; Weaviate v1.37 has built-in MCP server
findings.evidence: Weaviate blog April 21, 2026: 'Engram: Memory by Weaviate — a deep dive into our managed memory service for agents'. Weaviate 1.37 Release (April 23, 2026): 'built-in MCP Server (Preview) ... that lets LLMs and IDEs speak to your database natively'. ANI News June 6, 2026: 'Engram processes inputs asynchronously through an extract-transform-commit pipeline'. Weaviate also launched a Free Forever Tier on Weaviate Cloud.
findings.kai_relevance: Engram is cloud-managed (not self-hostable), making it a poor fit for Kai-1's on-premise VPS deployment, but Weaviate's MCP server is relevant as a vector storage backend for other frameworks.

findings.claim: Redis provides agent-memory-server (open source GitHub) and published long-term memory architecture guides; Redis Iris unifies caching, vectors, and memory
findings.evidence: Redis blog April 28, 2026: 'Long-Term Memory Architectures for AI Agents — this guide focuses on the architecture behind long-term memory'. Redis GitHub agent-memory-server: 'Fast and flexible memory for agents and AI applications using Redis'. Redis blog June 10, 2026: 'Real-time context engine: Fresh context for better AI agents' — Redis Iris. Feb 11, 2026: 'Relevance AI reduced vector search latency from 2 seconds to 10 milliseconds after migrating to Redis — a 99.5% improvement'.
findings.kai_relevance: Redis is best positioned as an infrastructure component (short-term memory/caching layer) rather than a full memory framework for Kai-1, but the agent-memory-server is a viable self-hosted option.

findings.claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the de facto standard for AI agent connectivity; 97M+ SDK downloads, 13k+ MCP servers; donated to Linux Foundation Dec 2025
findings.evidence: Lushbinary MCP Guide 2026: 'Over 97 million SDK downloads, 13,000+ MCP servers on GitHub'. 'Donated to the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation in December 2025'. n1n.ai: 'LangChain, CrewAI, LangGraph, and LlamaIndex have moved from experimental support to making MCP the default protocol for tool-calling'. Anthropic released MCP Nov 2024; now supported by OpenAI, Google, Microsoft.
findings.kai_relevance: MCP compatibility is table-stakes for any memory framework Kai-1 adopts — OMEGA, Mem0 (OpenMemory), Graphiti/Zep all offer MCP servers, ensuring tool-agnostic integration.

findings.claim: Agent Memory benchmarks landscape: LongMemEval (ICLR 2025, 500 Qs), LoCoMo (1,540 Qs), BEAM (1M/10M tokens) are the three standards; Bench'd provides independent verification
findings.evidence: Mem0 State of AI Agent Memory 2026: 'Three benchmarks now define the measurement landscape: LoCoMo (1,540 questions), LongMemEval (500 questions), BEAM (1M and 10M token scales)'. Bench'd: 'Mem0 managed platform self-reports 93.4% on LongMemEval and 66.9-68.5% on LOCOMO. Bench'd's independent test of Mem0's open-source edition scored 32.4% on LongMemEval'. Bench'd harness is open source (Ed25519-signed manifests). AgentOS methodology disclosure shows most vendors report 'S only' (single-session) rather than 'M' (multi-session) on LongMemEval.
findings.kai_relevance: Critical lesson for Kai-1: self-reported benchmarks (especially Mem0) may not reflect actual self-hosted OSS performance; independent verification via Bench'd is recommended before any framework decision.

findings.claim: Token efficiency varies wildly: OMEGA ~1,500 tokens/query, Zep ~5K-15K, Mem0 ~6.9K, Observational Memory ~70,000 tokens/query
findings.evidence: OMEGA cost analysis: 'OMEGA ~1,500 tokens/query (hybrid semantic + BM25, top 5-10 results). Zep/Graphiti ~5K-15K (graph query + entity extraction). Observational Memory ~70,000 (full memory dump into context block)'. Mem0 reports 6,956 tokens/query on LoCoMo. Claude Native ~800 tokens (MEMORY.md capped at 200 lines). Monthly cost projection at 10K sessions: OMEGA $150, Zep Cloud $1,000-1,475, Mem0 Pro $249+, Observational $7,000.
findings.kai_relevance: For Kai-1's 32GB VPS, token efficiency directly impacts inference cost and context window management: OMEGA's ~1,500 tokens/query is ideal; Obs.Memory's ~70K is prohibitive.

findings.claim: LongMemEval leaderboard (verified): OMEGA 95.4% (GPT-4.1), Mastra OM 94.87% (gpt-5-mini), Emergence 86%, Zep 71.2% (GPT-4o); Mem0 managed 94.4%, Mem0 OSS 32.4%
findings.evidence: OMEGA benchmarks page (April 2026): LongMemEval leaderboard — 1. OMEGA 95.4%, 2. Mastra OM 94.87% (gpt-5-mini, gpt-4o score: 84.23%), 3. Emergence AI 86%, 4. Zep/Graphiti 71.2%. Mem0 self-reports 94.4% (April 2026). Bench'd independent: Mem0 OSS 32.4%. AgentOS: 'Competitive with the strongest published M results in the LongMemEval paper' at 72%+ for multi-session. Note: different evaluation methodologies make direct cross-framework comparison unreliable without a common harness.
findings.kai_relevance: The LongMemEval leaderboard shows OMEGA and Mastra leading, but Kai-1 should benchmark candidates on its own Russian-language conversational data for an apples-to-apples comparison.

findings.claim: Agent memory stacks in 2026 split into two layers: thread-scoped checkpointing (LangGraph) and cross-session memory (Mem0/Letta/Zep); not one component
findings.evidence: BestAIWeb.ai May 2026: 'Agent State Management is no longer one decision — it's a layered architecture, with thread-scoped checkpointing at the bottom and cross-session memory on top'. 'LangGraph 1.0 became the production default for one specific job: keeping a single agent task alive through crashes, retries, and human-in-the-loop interrupts. It does not persist what the user told you yesterday.'
findings.kai_relevance: Kai-1 should architect memory as a separate layer from agent orchestration (LangGraph) rather than choosing a single monolithic framework, following 2026 production patterns.

gaps: No published LongMemEval scores for Letta, Cognee, or LangMem — cannot compare their benchmark performance on the standard metric
Mem0 OSS vs managed platform performance gap (32.4% vs 94.4% on LongMemEval) is documented but root cause not clearly explained in official sources
Redis agent-memory-server has no published benchmark scores on LongMemEval or LoCoMo
Weaviate Engram has no published LongMemEval/LoCoMo benchmark scores yet (product is very new, GA April 2026)
No direct comparison of Russian-language memory retrieval performance across frameworks — all benchmarks use English datasets
OMEGA's 18 MCP tools are listed but not individually documented in the comparison page; specific tool capabilities need further investigation
Pricing for Letta Cloud and Weaviate Cloud is not clearly published (contact-sales pricing)
LongMemEval leaderboard scores use different methodologies (task-averaged vs category-specific), making cross-framework comparison imprecise without a common harness like Bench'd
No memory framework has published benchmarks for 32GB VPS resource-constrained deployment scenarios similar to Kai-1
queries_used: Zep Graphiti temporal knowledge graph LongMemEval 2025 2026
Weaviate context engineering memory layer agent 2025 2026
